title: India Strengthens Strategies for Leprosy-Free Vision
summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare convened a regional workshop in Chhattisgarh to achieve zero leprosy transmission. While India attained national elimination as a public health problem in 2005, transmission persists in endemic hotspots. Officials emphasized early diagnosis, periodic case detection campaigns, and expanding Single-Dose Rifampicin coverage for eligible healthy contacts to interrupt the disease's transmission cycle effectively.

title: PMSMA Marks Decade of Care with Major Health Gains
summary: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda launched celebrations for 10 years of Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyaan. India achieved a significant 86% reduction in maternal mortality, outpacing the global average. Key health indicators show institutional deliveries have risen to 90.6%, while antenatal care coverage has reached 95.9%, reflecting the government's commitment to ensuring safe motherhood and healthy newborns.

title: West Bengal Joins Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Scheme
summary: West Bengal has become the 36th State or Union Territory to implement the Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY scheme. Following a Memorandum of Understanding signed between the National Health Authority and the state government, health assurance benefits will now extend to approximately 1.43 crore families. This landmark inclusion marks a major step toward achieving universal health coverage and financial protection for citizens.
